title: The Convenience Store at 3 AM
text_zh: 凌晨三点的便利店跟白天完全是两个世界。灯光还是那么亮，货架上的东西还是那么整齐，但是整个店只有我和收银员两个人。他是一个看起来二十出头的男孩，正在用笔记本写着什么。我拿了一个饭团和一瓶绿茶走过去结账。他抬起头，微微笑了一下。我注意到他写的是数学公式——大概是一个大学生在打工。我坐在便利店门口的台阶上吃饭团，街上一辆车都没有，只有路灯静静地亮着。远处偶尔传来一两声猫叫。我觉得凌晨三点的城市特别诚实，没有装饰，没有表演，只有最真实的安静。吃完饭团我回去的时候，那个男孩又低下头继续写他的数学题了。
text_pinyin: Língchén sān diǎn de biànlìdiàn gēn báitiān wánquán shì liǎng gè shìjiè. Dēngguāng háishì nàme liàng, huòjià shàng de dōngxi háishì nàme zhěngqí, dànshì zhěnggè diàn zhǐ yǒu wǒ hé shōuyín yuán liǎng gè rén. Tā shì yí gè kàn qǐlái èrshí chūtóu de nánhái, zhèngzài yòng bǐjìběn xiě zhe shénme. Wǒ ná le yí gè fàntuán hé yì píng lǜchá zǒu guòqù jiézhàng. Tā tái qǐ tóu, wēiwēi xiào le yíxià. Wǒ zhùyì dào tā xiě de shì shùxué gōngshì — dàgài shì yí gè dàxuéshēng zài dǎgōng. Wǒ zuò zài biànlìdiàn ménkǒu de táijiē shàng chī fàntuán, jiē shàng yí liàng chē dōu méiyǒu, zhǐ yǒu lùdēng jìngjìng de liàng zhe. Yuǎnchù ǒu'ěr chuán lái yì liǎng shēng māo jiào. Wǒ juéde língchén sān diǎn de chéngshì tèbié chéngshí, méiyǒu zhuāngshì, méiyǒu biǎoyǎn, zhǐ yǒu zuì zhēnshí de ānjìng. Chī wán fàntuán wǒ huíqù de shíhou, nàge nánhái yòu dī xià tóu jìxù xiě tā de shùxué tí le.
text_en: The convenience store at 3 AM is a completely different world from daytime. The lights are just as bright, the shelves just as tidy, but the entire store holds only me and the cashier. He's a young man who looks barely twenty, writing something in a notebook. I grabbed a rice ball and a bottle of green tea and went to check out. He looked up and gave a slight smile. I noticed he was writing math formulas — probably a college student working part-time. I sat on the steps outside the store eating my rice ball. Not a single car on the street, just streetlights shining quietly. An occasional cat call drifted from somewhere distant. I think the city at 3 AM is especially honest — no decoration, no performance, just the most authentic quiet. When I finished my rice ball and headed back, the young man had lowered his head again, continuing his math problems.

questions.q_zh: 收银员在写什么？
questions.q_en: What was the cashier writing?

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得凌晨三点的城市怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the author feel about the city at 3 AM?

options.pinyin: tài ānjìng le, ràng rén hàipà

options.text: 特别诚实，只有最真实的安静
options.pinyin: tèbié chéngshí, zhǐ yǒu zuì zhēnshí de ānjìng
options.text_en: especially honest, only the most authentic quiet

title: The Elevator That Gets Stuck
text_zh: 上个星期五，办公楼的电梯突然在七楼和八楼之间停了。里面只有我和一个不认识的女士。一开始我们都有点紧张，按了好几次紧急按钮。物业说十分钟就来修。那十分钟是我在这栋楼里跟陌生人说话最多的一次。她是十二楼一家设计公司的，来这栋楼上班三年了，之前从来没遇到过电梯坏。我说我在九楼，来了两年。我们聊了聊楼下那家咖啡店的咖啡越来越贵了，又聊了聊最近天气忽冷忽热。十分钟很快就过去了，电梯修好以后我们各自去了自己的楼层。但是从那以后，我们在大堂碰到的时候会互相笑一笑，说一声「你好」。原来，有时候需要一个小小的意外，才能让两个每天擦肩而过的人产生联系。
text_pinyin: Shàng gè xīngqīwǔ, bàngōnglóu de diàntī tūrán zài qī lóu hé bā lóu zhījiān tíng le. Lǐmiàn zhǐ yǒu wǒ hé yí gè bú rènshi de nǚshì. Yì kāishǐ wǒmen dōu yǒudiǎn jǐnzhāng, àn le hǎo jǐ cì jǐnjí ànniǔ. Wùyè shuō shí fēnzhōng jiù lái xiū. Nà shí fēnzhōng shì wǒ zài zhè dòng lóu lǐ gēn mòshēng rén shuō huà zuì duō de yí cì. Tā shì shí'èr lóu yì jiā shèjì gōngsī de, lái zhè dòng lóu shàng bān sān nián le, zhīqián cónglái méi yù dào guò diàntī huài. Wǒ shuō wǒ zài jiǔ lóu, lái le liǎng nián. Wǒmen liáo le liáo lóu xià nà jiā kāfēi diàn de kāfēi yuè lái yuè guì le, yòu liáo le liáo zuìjìn tiānqì hū lěng hū rè. Shí fēnzhōng hěn kuài jiù guòqù le, diàntī xiū hǎo yǐhòu wǒmen gèzì qù le zìjǐ de lóucéng. Dànshì cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒmen zài dàtáng pèng dào de shíhou huì hùxiāng xiào yí xiào, shuō yì shēng「nǐ hǎo」. Yuánlái, yǒu shíhou xūyào yí gè xiǎo xiǎo de yìwài, cái néng ràng liǎng gè měi tiān cā jiān ér guò de rén chǎnshēng liánxì.
text_en: Last Friday, the office elevator suddenly stopped between the seventh and eighth floors. Inside were just me and a woman I didn't know. At first we were both a bit nervous, pressing the emergency button several times. The property management said they'd come fix it in ten minutes. Those ten minutes were the most I've ever talked to a stranger in this building. She works at a design company on the twelfth floor, has been coming to this building for three years, and had never encountered an elevator breakdown before. I said I'm on the ninth floor, been here two years. We chatted about how the coffee at the shop downstairs keeps getting more expensive, and about the recent weather going back and forth between cold and hot. The ten minutes passed quickly, and after the elevator was fixed we each went to our own floors. But ever since then, when we run into each other in the lobby we exchange a smile and a 'hello.' It turns out that sometimes it takes a small accident to create a connection between two people who pass each other every day.

questions.q_zh: 电梯卡在了哪里？
questions.q_en: Where did the elevator get stuck?

options.pinyin: yī lóu hé èr lóu zhījiān
qī lóu hé bā lóu zhījiān
jiǔ lóu hé shí lóu zhījiān

questions.q_zh: 电梯修好以后他们的关系怎么样了？
questions.q_en: How is their relationship after the elevator was fixed?

options.text: 碰到的时候会笑一笑说你好
options.pinyin: pèng dào de shíhou huì xiào yí xiào shuō nǐ hǎo
options.text_en: they smile and say hello when they meet

title: The Cat Colony Under the Bridge
text_zh: 河边的那座旧桥下面住着一群猫。没有人知道它们是什么时候开始住在那里的，好像它们一直就在那里。每天傍晚，附近有两三个人会来喂它们。其中一个是开出租车的大叔，他下班以后总是先去桥下看一眼猫，再回家。他跟我说他一开始只是偶然看到了一只小猫，给它喂了一点鱼。后来那只猫每天都在同一个地方等他。再后来，更多的猫出现了。现在桥下大概有十几只猫，大的小的都有。它们好像已经习惯了城市的噪音，汽车开过去的时候也不怕。大叔说：「它们选择住在这里，这里就是它们的家。我只是一个邻居。」
text_pinyin: Hé biān de nà zuò jiù qiáo xiàmiàn zhù zhe yì qún māo. Méiyǒu rén zhīdào tāmen shì shénme shíhou kāishǐ zhù zài nàlǐ de, hǎoxiàng tāmen yìzhí jiù zài nàlǐ. Měi tiān bàngwǎn, fùjìn yǒu liǎng sān gè rén huì lái wèi tāmen. Qízhōng yí gè shì kāi chūzūchē de dàshū, tā xià bān yǐhòu zǒng shì xiān qù qiáo xià kàn yì yǎn māo, zài huí jiā. Tā gēn wǒ shuō tā yì kāishǐ zhǐ shì ǒurán kàn dào le yì zhī xiǎo māo, gěi tā wèi le yìdiǎn yú. Hòulái nà zhī māo měi tiān dōu zài tóng yí gè dìfang děng tā. Zài hòulái, gèng duō de māo chūxiàn le. Xiànzài qiáo xià dàgài yǒu shí jǐ zhī māo, dà de xiǎo de dōu yǒu. Tāmen hǎoxiàng yǐjīng xíguàn le chéngshì de zàoyīn, qìchē kāi guòqù de shíhou yě bú pà. Dàshū shuō: 「Tāmen xuǎnzé zhù zài zhèlǐ, zhèlǐ jiù shì tāmen de jiā. Wǒ zhǐ shì yí gè línjū.」
text_en: Under the old bridge by the river lives a colony of cats. No one knows when they first started living there — it's as if they've always been. Every evening, two or three people from the neighborhood come to feed them. One is a taxi driver who always stops by the bridge to check on the cats after work before going home. He told me that at first he just happened to see a small cat and fed it a bit of fish. Later that cat waited for him in the same spot every day. Then more cats appeared. Now there are about a dozen under the bridge, big and small. They seem to have gotten used to the city's noise, unfazed even when cars drive past. The driver said: 'They chose to live here, so this is their home. I'm just a neighbor.'

questions.q_zh: 出租车大叔一开始怎么认识那些猫的？
questions.q_en: How did the taxi driver first encounter the cats?

options.text: 偶然看到一只小猫，喂了一点鱼
options.pinyin: ǒurán kàn dào yì zhī xiǎo māo, wèi le yìdiǎn yú
options.text_en: happened to see a kitten and fed it fish

options.pinyin: tā zài qiáo xià jiǎn dào de
options.text_en: he found them under the bridge

questions.q_zh: 大叔觉得自己跟猫是什么关系？
questions.q_en: What does the driver consider his relationship with the cats?

options.pinyin: tā zhǐ shì yí gè línjū

title: Waiting in the Rain
text_zh: 我站在公交站等车，雨已经下了快一个小时了。旁边站着一个老爷爷，他撑着一把很旧的黑伞，伞上有几个小洞，雨水从洞里滴下来。他好像一点也不着急，安安静静地看着雨。我问他要不要站到棚子里面来，他摇摇头说：「在雨里站一会儿也不错。」他告诉我他已经退休了，平时没什么事情做，下雨的时候他就出来走走。他说他小时候住在农村，下雨的时候孩子们都在外面玩，现在的孩子下雨就待在家里看手机，不一样了。公交车终于来了，我让老爷爷先上车。他上车的时候收起伞，对我点了点头。雨水顺着他的伞流了一地。那个画面很简单，但是在我的记忆里留了很久。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhàn zài gōngjiāo zhàn děng chē, yǔ yǐjīng xià le kuài yí gè xiǎoshí le. Pángbiān zhàn zhe yí gè lǎo yéye, tā chēng zhe yì bǎ hěn jiù de hēi sǎn, sǎn shàng yǒu jǐ gè xiǎo dòng, yǔshuǐ cóng dòng lǐ dī xiàlái. Tā hǎoxiàng yìdiǎn yě bù zháojí, ān'ānjìngjìng de kàn zhe yǔ. Wǒ wèn tā yào bu yào zhàn dào péngzi lǐmiàn lái, tā yáoyao tóu shuō: 「Zài yǔ lǐ zhàn yíhuìr yě búcuò.」 Tā gàosu wǒ tā yǐjīng tuìxiū le, píngshí méi shénme shìqing zuò, xià yǔ de shíhou tā jiù chūlái zǒuzou. Tā shuō tā xiǎo shíhou zhù zài nóngcūn, xià yǔ de shíhou háizimen dōu zài wàimiàn wán, xiànzài de háizi xià yǔ jiù dāi zài jiā lǐ kàn shǒujī, bù yíyàng le. Gōngjiāo chē zhōngyú lái le, wǒ ràng lǎo yéye xiān shàng chē. Tā shàng chē de shíhou shōu qǐ sǎn, duì wǒ diǎn le diǎn tóu. Yǔshuǐ shùn zhe tā de sǎn liú le yí dì. Nàge huàmiàn hěn jiǎndān, dànshì zài wǒ de jìyì lǐ liú le hěn jiǔ.
text_en: I stood at the bus stop waiting, and it had been raining for nearly an hour. Beside me stood an old grandfather holding a very old black umbrella with a few small holes — rainwater dripped through them. He didn't seem rushed at all, quietly watching the rain. I asked if he wanted to come stand under the shelter. He shook his head and said: 'Standing in the rain for a bit isn't bad.' He told me he's retired and doesn't have much to do — when it rains, he comes out for a walk. He said when he was young and lived in the countryside, children played outside in the rain, but nowadays children stay home and look at their phones when it rains — things are different now. The bus finally came, and I let the grandfather board first. As he got on, he folded his umbrella and nodded at me. Rainwater ran off his umbrella onto the ground. It was a simple scene, but it stayed in my memory for a long time.

questions.q_zh: 老爷爷为什么不站到棚子里？
questions.q_en: Why didn't the grandfather stand under the shelter?

options.text: 他觉得在雨里站一会儿也不错
options.pinyin: tā juéde zài yǔ lǐ zhàn yíhuìr yě búcuò
options.text_en: he felt standing in the rain was nice

questions.q_zh: 老爷爷小时候下雨的时候做什么？
questions.q_en: What did the grandfather do in the rain as a child?

title: The Museum Guard's Favorite Painting
text_zh: 我在美术馆里遇到了一个很特别的保安。大部分保安只是站在角落里看手机，但是他不一样。他会站在不同的画前面看，好像他也是一个参观者。有一次我问他：「你最喜欢哪一幅画？」他想了想，带我走到了二楼最里面的一个小展厅。那里挂着一幅不太大的画，画的是一个黄昏的海边，沙滩上只有一个人的影子。他说：「我在这里工作了五年，每天都会来看这幅画。它让我想起我老家的海。」他老家在南方的一个海边小城，他年轻的时候来了这个城市打工，已经十几年没回去了。他说有时候站在这幅画前面，就好像回到了小时候在海边玩的日子。我觉得，对于他来说，这幅画不只是一幅画，而是一扇窗户，能看到他最想念的风景。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài měishùguǎn lǐ yù dào le yí gè hěn tèbié de bǎo'ān. Dà bùfen bǎo'ān zhǐ shì zhàn zài jiǎoluò lǐ kàn shǒujī, dànshì tā bù yíyàng. Tā huì zhàn zài bùtóng de huà qiánmiàn kàn, hǎoxiàng tā yě shì yí gè cānguān zhě. Yǒu yí cì wǒ wèn tā: 「Nǐ zuì xǐhuan nǎ yì fú huà?」 Tā xiǎng le xiǎng, dài wǒ zǒu dào le èr lóu zuì lǐmiàn de yí gè xiǎo zhǎntīng. Nàlǐ guà zhe yì fú bú tài dà de huà, huà de shì yí gè huánghūn de hǎibiān, shātān shàng zhǐ yǒu yí gè rén de yǐngzi. Tā shuō: 「Wǒ zài zhèlǐ gōngzuò le wǔ nián, měi tiān dōu huì lái kàn zhè fú huà. Tā ràng wǒ xiǎng qǐ wǒ lǎojiā de hǎi.」 Tā lǎojiā zài nánfāng de yí gè hǎibiān xiǎo chéng, tā niánqīng de shíhou lái le zhège chéngshì dǎgōng, yǐjīng shí jǐ nián méi huíqù le. Tā shuō yǒu shíhou zhàn zài zhè fú huà qiánmiàn, jiù hǎoxiàng huí dào le xiǎo shíhou zài hǎibiān wán de rìzi. Wǒ juéde, duìyú tā lái shuō, zhè fú huà bù zhǐ shì yì fú huà, ér shì yí shàn chuānghu, néng kàn dào tā zuì xiǎngniàn de fēngjǐng.
text_en: I met a very special security guard at the art museum. Most guards just stand in corners looking at their phones, but he's different. He stands in front of different paintings and looks at them, as if he's also a visitor. Once I asked him: 'Which painting is your favorite?' He thought about it, then led me to a small exhibition room at the far end of the second floor. There hung a modest painting of a seaside at dusk, with only one person's shadow on the beach. He said: 'I've worked here for five years, and I come to see this painting every day. It reminds me of the sea back home.' His hometown is a small coastal city in the south. He came to this city to work when he was young and hasn't been back in over ten years. He says sometimes standing before this painting feels like returning to his childhood days playing by the sea. I think for him, this painting isn't just a painting — it's a window through which he can see the scenery he misses most.

questions.q_zh: 那幅画画的是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the painting depict?

options.text: 黄昏的海边，沙滩上有一个人的影子
options.pinyin: huánghūn de hǎibiān, shātān shàng yǒu yí gè rén de yǐngzi
options.text_en: a seaside at dusk with one person's shadow on the beach

questions.q_zh: 保安多长时间没回老家了？
questions.q_en: How long has the guard been away from his hometown?

title: The Barista's Latte Art
text_zh: 我发现公司附近新开了一家咖啡店，里面的咖啡师特别喜欢做拉花。每一杯拿铁上面都有不同的图案——有时候是一片叶子，有时候是一颗心，有时候是一只猫。我问他是怎么学会的，他说他每天下班以后都会练习两个小时。一开始总是失败，牛奶倒下去就变成一团白色的东西，什么也看不出来。但是他没有放弃，练了半年以后终于可以画出漂亮的图案了。他说最开心的时刻不是客人夸他，而是看到客人拿到咖啡以后，先拿出手机拍一张照片，然后才舍得喝。他笑着说：「那一秒钟的犹豫，就是对我最大的称赞。」我觉得他说得很有道理。有些美好的东西，就是因为短暂才让人珍惜。
text_pinyin: Wǒ fāxiàn gōngsī fùjìn xīn kāi le yì jiā kāfēi diàn, lǐmiàn de kāfēi shī tèbié xǐhuan zuò lāhuā. Měi yì bēi ná tiě shàngmiàn dōu yǒu bùtóng de tú'àn — yǒu shíhou shì yí piàn yèzi, yǒu shíhou shì yì kē xīn, yǒu shíhou shì yì zhī māo. Wǒ wèn tā shì zěnme xué huì de, tā shuō tā měi tiān xià bān yǐhòu dōu huì liànxí liǎng gè xiǎoshí. Yì kāishǐ zǒng shì shībài, niúnǎi dào xiàqù jiù biàn chéng yì tuán bái sè de dōngxi, shénme yě kàn bu chūlái. Dànshì tā méiyǒu fàngqì, liàn le bàn nián yǐhòu zhōngyú kěyǐ huà chū piàoliang de tú'àn le. Tā shuō zuì kāixīn de shíkè bú shì kèrén kuā tā, ér shì kàn dào kèrén ná dào kāfēi yǐhòu, xiān ná chū shǒujī pāi yì zhāng zhàopiàn, ránhòu cái shěde hē. Tā xiào zhe shuō: 「Nà yì miǎozhōng de yóuyù, jiù shì duì wǒ zuì dà de chēngzàn.」 Wǒ juéde tā shuō de hěn yǒu dàolǐ. Yǒuxiē měihǎo de dōngxi, jiù shì yīnwèi duǎnzàn cái ràng rén zhēnxī.
text_en: I discovered a new coffee shop near the office where the barista especially loves making latte art. Each latte has a different design — sometimes a leaf, sometimes a heart, sometimes a cat. I asked how he learned. He said he practices for two hours after work every day. At first he always failed — the milk poured in would just become a white blob with no recognizable shape. But he didn't give up, and after six months of practice he could finally create beautiful patterns. He said his happiest moment isn't when customers compliment him, but when he sees a customer receive their coffee, pull out their phone to take a photo first, and only then bear to drink it. He smiled and said: 'That one second of hesitation is the greatest compliment to me.' I think he makes a good point. Some beautiful things are precious precisely because they're brief.

questions.q_zh: 咖啡师练了多长时间才学会拉花？
questions.q_en: How long did the barista practice before learning latte art?

questions.q_zh: 咖啡师最开心的是什么时刻？
questions.q_en: What is the barista's happiest moment?

options.text: 客人先拍照再喝的那一秒犹豫
options.pinyin: kèrén xiān pāi zhào zài hē de nà yì miǎo yóuyù
options.text_en: the second of hesitation when customers photo first

title: The Apartment Next Door
text_zh: 住在我隔壁的是一对年轻夫妻。我们的墙不太隔音，所以有时候能听到他们的生活。每天早上六点半，闹钟响了以后，他们会小声地讨论今天谁先用洗手间。做早饭的时候，厨房里传来锅碗的声音和炒蛋的香味。晚上的时候，有时候能听到他们一起看电影，偶尔传来笑声。周末的早上他们不设闹钟，我能感觉到整面墙都安静下来了。有一次在电梯里碰到他们，女孩有点不好意思地说：「我们是不是太吵了？」我笑着摇摇头说不会。其实，听到隔壁有人好好地生活着，会让我觉得自己也没有那么孤单。
text_pinyin: Zhù zài wǒ gébì de shì yí duì niánqīng fūqī. Wǒmen de qiáng bú tài géyīn, suǒyǐ yǒu shíhou néng tīng dào tāmen de shēnghuó. Měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn, nàozhōng xiǎng le yǐhòu, tāmen huì xiǎo shēng de tǎolùn jīntiān shéi xiān yòng xǐshǒujiān. Zuò zǎofàn de shíhou, chúfáng lǐ chuán lái guō wǎn de shēngyīn hé chǎo dàn de xiāngwèi. Wǎnshang de shíhou, yǒu shíhou néng tīng dào tāmen yìqǐ kàn diànyǐng, ǒu'ěr chuán lái xiào shēng. Zhōumò de zǎoshang tāmen bú shè nàozhōng, wǒ néng gǎnjué dào zhěng miàn qiáng dōu ānjìng xiàlái le. Yǒu yí cì zài diàntī lǐ pèng dào tāmen, nǚhái yǒudiǎn bù hǎoyìsi de shuō: 「Wǒmen shì bu shì tài chǎo le?」 Wǒ xiào zhe yáoyao tóu shuō bú huì. Qíshí, tīng dào gébì yǒu rén hǎohǎo de shēnghuó zhe, huì ràng wǒ juéde zìjǐ yě méiyǒu nàme gūdān.
text_en: A young couple lives next door to me. Our walls aren't very soundproof, so sometimes I can hear their daily life. Every morning at six-thirty, after the alarm goes off, they quietly discuss who gets the bathroom first. When they make breakfast, the sounds of pots and pans and the smell of scrambled eggs drift from the kitchen. In the evenings, I can sometimes hear them watching a movie together, with occasional laughter. On weekend mornings they don't set an alarm, and I can feel the entire wall go quiet. Once I ran into them in the elevator, and the girl said a bit sheepishly: 'Are we too noisy?' I smiled, shook my head, and said no. Actually, hearing people living well next door makes me feel less alone myself.

questions.q_zh: 每天早上闹钟响了以后他们讨论什么？
questions.q_en: What do they discuss after the alarm goes off?

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么不觉得隔壁吵？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't 'I' find the neighbors noisy?

options.text: 听到有人好好生活让自己不那么孤单
options.pinyin: tīng dào yǒu rén hǎohǎo shēnghuó ràng zìjǐ bú nàme gūdān
options.text_en: hearing people living well makes one less lonely

title: The Rooftop at Night
text_zh: 夏天的晚上，我喜欢一个人上楼顶坐一会儿。楼顶上什么都没有，只有水泥地和一些旧管道。但是从这里可以看到很远的地方——对面的楼、远处的马路、更远处闪着灯光的高楼。晚上的风吹过来很凉快，城市的声音到了这个高度变得很模糊，就像一首很远的歌。有时候我会带一罐啤酒上来，有时候什么都不带，就这样坐着看城市的灯光。我想，城市里的每一盏灯后面都有一个人，他们各自在过着自己的生活。有的人在加班，有的人在做饭，有的人在哄孩子睡觉。这些灯光让我觉得，虽然我不认识他们，但是我们在同一个夜晚里一起存在着。
text_pinyin: Xiàtiān de wǎnshang, wǒ xǐhuan yí gè rén shàng lóudǐng zuò yíhuìr. Lóudǐng shàng shénme dōu méiyǒu, zhǐ yǒu shuǐní dì hé yìxiē jiù guǎndào. Dànshì cóng zhèlǐ kěyǐ kàn dào hěn yuǎn de dìfang — duìmiàn de lóu, yuǎnchù de mǎlù, gèng yuǎnchù shǎn zhe dēngguāng de gāolóu. Wǎnshang de fēng chuī guòlái hěn liángkuai, chéngshì de shēngyīn dào le zhège gāodù biàn de hěn móhu, jiù xiàng yì shǒu hěn yuǎn de gē. Yǒu shíhou wǒ huì dài yì guàn píjiǔ shànglái, yǒu shíhou shénme dōu bú dài, jiù zhèyàng zuòzhe kàn chéngshì de dēngguāng. Wǒ xiǎng, chéngshì lǐ de měi yì zhǎn dēng hòumiàn dōu yǒu yí gè rén, tāmen gèzì zài guò zhe zìjǐ de shēnghuó. Yǒu de rén zài jiābān, yǒu de rén zài zuò fàn, yǒu de rén zài hǒng háizi shuìjiào. Zhèxiē dēngguāng ràng wǒ juéde, suīrán wǒ bú rènshi tāmen, dànshì wǒmen zài tóng yí gè yèwǎn lǐ yìqǐ cúnzài zhe.
text_en: On summer evenings, I like to go up to the rooftop alone and sit for a while. There's nothing up there — just concrete and some old pipes. But from here you can see very far — the buildings across the way, the distant roads, and even farther, skyscrapers twinkling with lights. The night breeze is cool, and the city's sounds at this height become muffled, like a distant song. Sometimes I bring a can of beer up, sometimes nothing at all — I just sit and watch the city lights. I think behind every light in the city is a person, each living their own life. Some are working overtime, some are cooking, some are coaxing children to sleep. These lights make me feel that although I don't know them, we exist together in the same night.

questions.q_zh: 城市的声音到了楼顶变成了什么样？
questions.q_en: What do city sounds become at rooftop height?

options.text: 很模糊，像一首很远的歌
options.pinyin: hěn móhu, xiàng yì shǒu hěn yuǎn de gē

questions.q_zh: 城市的灯光让作者有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How do the city lights make the author feel?

options.text: 跟不认识的人在同一个夜晚一起存在
options.pinyin: gēn bú rènshi de rén zài tóng yí gè yèwǎn yìqǐ cúnzài
options.text_en: existing together with strangers in the same night

title: The Saturday Farmers Market Musician
text_zh: 每个周六的农贸市场上，除了卖菜的和买菜的，还有一个拉二胡的老人。他总是坐在市场入口旁边的一把塑料椅子上，面前放着一个旧帽子。他拉的曲子都很老，是那种我小时候在电视里听过的旋律。来来往往的人大部分不会停下来听，但是他好像并不在意。他闭着眼睛拉琴，沉浸在自己的音乐里。有一次我买完菜以后在他旁边站了一会儿，听他拉了两首曲子。他睁开眼睛看到我，笑着点了点头，然后继续拉下一首。我在帽子里放了一些零钱。他说了声谢谢，然后又闭上了眼睛。市场的嘈杂声和他的音乐混在一起，变成了一种奇怪的和谐。
text_pinyin: Měi gè zhōuliù de nóngmào shìchǎng shàng, chúle mài cài de hé mǎi cài de, hái yǒu yí gè lā èrhú de lǎorén. Tā zǒng shì zuò zài shìchǎng rùkǒu pángbiān de yì bǎ sùliào yǐzi shàng, miànqián fàng zhe yí gè jiù màozi. Tā lā de qǔzi dōu hěn lǎo, shì nà zhǒng wǒ xiǎo shíhou zài diànshì lǐ tīng guò de xuánlǜ. Láilái wǎngwǎng de rén dà bùfen bú huì tíng xiàlái tīng, dànshì tā hǎoxiàng bìng bú zàiyì. Tā bì zhe yǎnjing lā qín, chénjìn zài zìjǐ de yīnyuè lǐ. Yǒu yí cì wǒ mǎi wán cài yǐhòu zài tā pángbiān zhàn le yíhuìr, tīng tā lā le liǎng shǒu qǔzi. Tā zhēng kāi yǎnjing kàn dào wǒ, xiào zhe diǎn le diǎn tóu, ránhòu jìxù lā xià yì shǒu. Wǒ zài màozi lǐ fàng le yìxiē língqián. Tā shuō le shēng xièxie, ránhòu yòu bì shàng le yǎnjing. Shìchǎng de cáozá shēng hé tā de yīnyuè hùn zài yìqǐ, biàn chéng le yì zhǒng qíguài de héxié.
text_en: At the Saturday farmers market, besides the vendors and shoppers, there's an old man who plays the erhu. He always sits on a plastic chair near the market entrance, with an old hat placed in front of him. The tunes he plays are all old ones — the kind of melodies I heard on TV as a child. Most passersby don't stop to listen, but he doesn't seem to mind. He plays with his eyes closed, immersed in his own music. Once after buying groceries, I stood beside him for a while and listened to two pieces. He opened his eyes, saw me, smiled and nodded, then continued with the next piece. I put some change in his hat. He said thank you, then closed his eyes again. The noise of the market and his music blended together into a strange kind of harmony.

questions.q_zh: 老人用什么乐器演奏？
questions.q_en: What instrument does the old man play?

questions.q_zh: 大部分路过的人会停下来听吗？
questions.q_en: Do most passersby stop to listen?

options.pinyin: dà bùfen rén huì tíng xiàlái
dà bùfen rén bú huì tíng xiàlái
suǒyǒu rén dōu huì tíng xiàlái

title: The Bookshop Cat
text_zh: 巷子里有一家很小的独立书店，门口总是坐着一只橘色的猫。它不怕人，谁走过来它都不动，好像觉得自己才是这条街的主人。进了书店你会发现，这只猫在里面也有一个固定的位置——靠窗的书架旁边有一个小垫子，那是它下午睡觉的地方。老板说这只猫是几年前自己跑过来的，从那以后就再也没离开过。有些客人来这家书店不是为了买书，而是为了看猫。它有时候会跳到收银台上面，用爪子轻轻碰你的手，好像在提醒你该买一本书了。老板笑着说：「这只猫比我更会做生意。」我觉得，一家有猫的书店，总是会让人多待一会儿。
text_pinyin: Xiàngzi lǐ yǒu yì jiā hěn xiǎo de dúlì shūdiàn, ménkǒu zǒng shì zuò zhe yì zhī jú sè de māo. Tā bú pà rén, shéi zǒu guòlái tā dōu bú dòng, hǎoxiàng juéde zìjǐ cái shì zhè tiáo jiē de zhǔrén. Jìn le shūdiàn nǐ huì fāxiàn, zhè zhī māo zài lǐmiàn yě yǒu yí gè gùdìng de wèizhì — kào chuāng de shūjià pángbiān yǒu yí gè xiǎo diànzi, nà shì tā xiàwǔ shuìjiào de dìfang. Lǎobǎn shuō zhè zhī māo shì jǐ nián qián zìjǐ pǎo guòlái de, cóng nà yǐhòu jiù zài yě méi líkāi guò. Yǒuxiē kèrén lái zhè jiā shūdiàn bú shì wèile mǎi shū, ér shì wèile kàn māo. Tā yǒu shíhou huì tiào dào shōuyíntái shàngmiàn, yòng zhuǎzi qīngqīng pèng nǐ de shǒu, hǎoxiàng zài tíxǐng nǐ gāi mǎi yì běn shū le. Lǎobǎn xiào zhe shuō: 「Zhè zhī māo bǐ wǒ gèng huì zuò shēngyì.」 Wǒ juéde, yì jiā yǒu māo de shūdiàn, zǒng shì huì ràng rén duō dāi yíhuìr.
text_en: In the alley there's a tiny independent bookshop with an orange cat always sitting at the entrance. It's not afraid of people — it doesn't move no matter who walks by, as if it considers itself the owner of this street. Inside the shop, you'll find the cat also has a designated spot — a small cushion beside the window bookshelf, where it naps in the afternoon. The owner says the cat showed up on its own a few years ago and has never left since. Some customers come to this bookshop not for books, but for the cat. It sometimes jumps onto the counter and gently taps your hand with its paw, as if reminding you it's time to buy a book. The owner laughs and says: 'This cat is better at business than I am.' I think a bookshop with a cat always makes people want to stay a little longer.

questions.q_zh: 猫下午在书店里哪里睡觉？
questions.q_en: Where does the cat nap in the afternoon?

options.text: 靠窗的书架旁边的小垫子上
options.pinyin: kào chuāng de shūjià pángbiān de xiǎo diànzi shàng
options.text_en: on a cushion by the window bookshelf

questions.q_zh: 老板说猫比他更会做什么？
questions.q_en: What does the owner say the cat is better at?
